name: Bazaar Inventory — Typosquat + Concentration (two distinct findings)
details: Bazaar inventory: 50536 listings across 762 unique hosts. TYPOSQUAT FINDING: 10 clusters (largest=8, 28 hosts in clusters), 0 non-ASCII hostnames. CONCENTRATION FINDING (separate semantics): top-host concentration 71.9% — marketplace-diversity signal, NOT typosquat signal.

typosquat_finding.interpretation: Levenshtein-clustered near-duplicate hostnames in the registry. Indicates marketplace lacks pre-list edit-distance defense.

concentration_finding.interpretation: Fraction of all listings registered by the top single hostname. Marketplace diversity signal — NOT a typosquat signal. High concentration suggests one operator dominates the registry, which is its own supply-chain consideration but distinct from near-duplicate hostname squatting.

csg_mapping: HC-7: Marketplace inventory must be auditable for typosquats
